metadata.description: hashlib 模块: hashlib.md5/sha1/sha256. The `update()` method accumulates data. The `hexdigest()` method produces a hexadecimal digest. Password hashing uses bcrypt. Here are brief Python code examples for each: ```python import hashlib # Example using md5 md5_hash = hashlib.md5() md5_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(md5_hash.hexdigest()) # Example using sha1 sha1_hash = hashlib.sha1() sha1_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(sha1_hash.hexdigest()) # Example using sha256 sha256_hash = hashlib.sha256() sha256_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(sha256_hash.hexdigest()) ``` For bcrypt, you would typically use a library like `bcrypt`: ```python import bcrypt password = b"supersecret" # Generate a salt and hash the password hashed_password = bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt()) print(hashed_password) ```

Here are brief Python code examples for each: ```python import hashlib # Example using md5 md5_hash = hashlib.md5() md5_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(md5_hash.hexdigest()) # Example using sha1 sha1_hash = hashlib.sha1() sha1_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(sha1_hash.hexdigest()) # Example using sha256 sha256_hash = hashlib.sha256() sha256_hash.update(b"Hello World") print(sha256_hash.hexdigest()) ``` For bcrypt, you would typically use a library like `bcrypt`: ```python import bcrypt password = b"supersecret" # Generate a salt and hash the password hashed_password = bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt()) print(hashed_password) ```
